    "IsA": [
      "それはボードウォーゲームです。",
      "それはゲームです。",
      "それはクアドリゲームです。",
      "それはフォリオゲームです。"
    ],

      "それは2人のプレイヤーがいます。",
      "それは機械化部隊を持っています。",
      "それは100個のカウンターを持っています。",
      "それは比較的少ないルールを持っています。",

      "それはキャンペーンゲームを持っています。",
      "それは地雷原を持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それはシンプルであるという特徴があります。",
      "それは遊びやすいという特徴があります。",
      "それは短い時間で完了できるという特徴があります。",
      "それは「イゴー、ユーゴー」交互ターンシステムという特徴があります。",
      "それはユニットのスタッキングが許可されないという特徴があります。",
      "それはユニットが敵の支配領域に入ると停止しなければならないという特徴があります。",
      "それは敗者が後退できない場合にのみ排除されるという特徴があります。",
      "それは通常のクアッドゲームよりもはるかに長いという特徴があります。",
      "それは流動的で機敏なアクションという特徴があります。",
      "それは序盤の動きがしばしば決定的なものになるという特徴があります。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それはガザラの戦いをシミュレートするために使用されます。",
      "それは第二次世界大戦中の戦闘をシミュレートするために使用されます。",
      "それは枢軸国軍を制御するために使用されます。",
      "それはイギリス軍を制御するために使用されます。",
      "それは機動のために使用されます。",
      "それはユニットを包囲するために機動するために使用されます。",
      "それはキャンペーンゲームを形成するためにシナリオをリンクするために使用されます。",
      "それは反撃するために使用されます。",
      "それは良好な状態で撤退するために使用されます。"
    ],

    ],

    ],

    ],

HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being simple.
It is characterized by being easy to play.
It is characterized by requiring a short period of time to complete.
It is characterized by an "I Go, You Go" alternating turn system.
It is characterized by units not allowed to stack.
It is characterized by a unit stopping if it enters an enemy's zone of control.
It is characterized by the loser being eliminated only if it can't retreat.
It is characterized by being much longer than usual Quad games.
It is characterized by fluid and mobile action.
It is characterized by the opening moves often proving decisive.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating the Battle of Gazala.
It is used for simulating battles during World War II.
It is used for controlling Axis forces.
It is used for controlling British forces.
It is used for maneuvering to surround the unit.
It is used for linking scenarios together to form a campaign game.
It is used for withdrawing in good order.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the North African campaign.
It originated in the Battle of Gazala.
CreatedBy: It was created by Howard Barasch.
PublishedIn: It was published as part of the Four Battles in North Africa quadrigame.
It was published as an individual folio game.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Simulations Publications Inc.
ConsistsOf: It consists of four games simulating four separate battles.
It consists of the four games Crusader, Supercharge, Kasserine and Cauldron.
PartOf: It is part of the Four Battles in North Africa quadrigame.
It is part of a gamebox containing four games.
It is part of a cardstock folio.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Battle of Gazala.
It is associated with World War II.
It is associated with the North African campaign.
It is associated with Tobruk.
It is associated with Erwin Rommel.
It is associated with "I Go, You Go" alternating turn system.
It is associated with SPI's previous publication The Battle of Borodino.
It is associated with minefields.
It is associated with The Comprehensive Guide to Board Wargaming.
It is associated with Fire & Movement magazine.
It is associated with Campaign magazine.
NamedAfter: It is named after the Battle of Gazala.
About: It is about the Battle of Gazala.
It is about the North African campaign.
It is about World War II.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by SPI's first quadrigame Blue & Gray.
It was influenced by The Battle of Borodino.
